product_data.Features: 65-inch OLED evo panel, 4K 120Hz, Dolby Vision IQ, Dolby Atmos, webOS 23, Game Optimizer with 4x HDMI 2.1 ports, AI Picture Pro

conversation_history.speaker: Buyer
conversation_history.message:
# Message to Seller

Hey! I came across your listing for the LG C3 65-inch OLED TV and I'm really interested in it. The specs look fantastic, and it's exactly what I've been looking for.

I was wondering if there's any flexibility on the price? I'm a serious buyer ready to move quickly, and I'd love to see if we can work something out that works for both of us.

What's the best you could do on this one?

Thanks!

---

**Why this works:**
- ✅ Friendly and natural opening
- ✅ Shows genuine interest in the product
- ✅ Mentions urgency ("ready to move quickly") to motivate the seller
- ✅ Opens negotiation without being aggressive
- ✅ Keeps it short and conversational
- ✅ Doesn't reveal your budget upfront (leaves room for negotiation)

conversation_history.speaker: Seller
conversation_history.message:
Thanks for reaching out! I'm glad you're interested in the C3 – it's an incredible TV, and I can tell you're someone who appreciates quality.

I've got it priced at $1,799, which is actually competitive for this model given the specs. That said, I do appreciate serious buyers. Here's what I can do: if you're ready to move forward today, I can bring it down to $1,650. That's a solid discount, and it reflects the value you're getting with that OLED evo panel and the full feature set.

Does that work for you, or would you like to discuss further?

conversation_history.speaker: Buyer
conversation_history.message: I appreciate the offer, but I need to be upfront with you – my budget is $880, so $1,650 is still well beyond what I can spend. I'm a serious buyer ready to move today if we can work something out in that range. Is there any flexibility there, or would you have any older stock, display models, or refurbished units that might fit my budget?

conversation_history.speaker: Seller
conversation_history.message:
I appreciate your honesty, and I understand budget constraints are real. However, I need to be straight with you too – $880 is below what I can work with on this unit. My absolute floor on a brand new C3 is $1,100, which is already a significant cut from retail.

That said, your question about alternatives is a good one. If you're open to it, I'd recommend:
- Checking if I have any B-stock or open-box C3s (sometimes available at better pricing)
- Looking at the LG B3 OLED as an alternative – similar quality, slightly fewer features, and typically $200-300 less

But if it's the C3 specifically and new condition, $1,100 is genuinely my lowest. Would either of those alternatives interest you, or should we explore other options?

conversation_history.speaker: Buyer
conversation_history.message:
I appreciate your honesty and the alternatives. Unfortunately, $1,100 is still more than double my budget, so a brand new C3 won't work for me at any price point you can offer.

Do you have any B-stock or open-box C3s available? That's really my best shot at getting this model. If not, I'd be interested in hearing more about that B3 OLED – what's the lowest you could do on one of those in new condition?

conversation_history.speaker: Seller
conversation_history.message:
I appreciate you being realistic about this. Unfortunately, I don't have any B-stock or open-box C3s in stock right now, but I can check with my warehouse and get back to you if that changes.

As for the B3 OLED – that's a solid alternative. New condition, I could do $950 on that one. It's got the same OLED picture quality and most of the gaming features, just without some of the advanced AI processing and a couple fewer HDMI 2.1 ports. Still an exceptional TV for the price.

That said, if your budget is genuinely capped at $880, even the B3 might be a stretch. Would $950 work for you, or should we look at other brands/sizes that might fit your actual budget better?

conversation_history.speaker: Buyer
conversation_history.message:
I appreciate you working with me, but $950 is still $70 over my hard budget of $880. I can't stretch beyond that – it's just not possible for me.

At this point, I think we're looking at different price points. If you do find any B-stock or open-box C3s, or if you have any other OLED options that might come in closer to $880, I'd definitely want to hear about it. Otherwise, I may need to explore other brands or look for a smaller screen size. Can you let me know if anything changes on your end?

conversation_history.speaker: Seller
conversation_history.message:
I totally understand – budget is budget, and I respect that you're being clear about your limits.

Here's the honest truth: at $880, we're outside the range for any new OLED TVs I can offer, whether it's the C3 or B3. If something opens up on the B-stock/open-box side, I'll absolutely reach out.

In the meantime, if you're flexible on brand or willing to go smaller (like a 55-inch OLED), I might have some options. Otherwise, you may indeed find better luck with other retailers or looking at non-OLED alternatives in your range.

I'll keep your info and contact you if anything changes. Fair enough?

conversation_history.speaker: Buyer
conversation_history.message:
Fair enough. I appreciate you being straight with me about what's realistic. Yes, please do reach out if any B-stock or open-box units come available – I'm genuinely interested in the C3 if the price works out.

In the meantime, I'll explore other options, but keep me in the loop. Thanks for being upfront throughout this conversation.

conversation_history.speaker: Seller
conversation_history.message:
Absolutely, I will. I've got your info and I'll definitely reach out if anything opens up on the B-stock or open-box side – no promises, but I'll keep an eye out.

Thanks for being a straight shooter too. Best of luck with your search, and I hope we can make something work down the road. Take care!

conversation_history.speaker: Buyer
conversation_history.message: Thanks, I appreciate it. Looking forward to hearing from you if something comes through. Take care!

conversation_history.speaker: Seller
conversation_history.message: You got it! I'll be in touch. Cheers!
